text: Clean code is easier to read and maintain. One important practice is using clear naming conventions: variable and function names should describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `calc`. Functions should be short and do only one thing. This makes the code simpler to test and modify later. For example, instead of writing one long block of code, you can split it into small functions, each handling a specific task. Finally, consistent formatting, like proper indentation and spacing, helps others understand your code quickly. When code is readable and well-organised, it saves time for everyone who works on it.
